---
format: 
  html:
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 3
---
# Francisco Garre-Frutos
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
[{{< fa globe >}} Website](https://franfrutos.github.io/){.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm role="button" data-toggle="data" title="Website"} [{{< fa envelope >}} Email](mailto:fgfrutos@ugr.es){.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm role="button" data-toggle="data" title="email"} [{{< fa brands twitter >}} Twitter](https://www.twitter.com/Fran_gfr){.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm role="button" data-toggle="data" title="Twitter"} [{{< fa brands github >}} Github](https://www.github.io/franfrutos){.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm role="button" data-toggle="data" title="Github"} [{{< fa brands bluesky >}} Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/frangfr.bsky.social){.btn .btn-primary .btn-sm role="button" data-toggle="data" title="Bluesky"}
:::

## <i class="fa-solid fa-graduation-cap"></i> Education

-   2021--Present \| **Ph.D.** *Experimental Psychology*, University of Granada. 

    - Ph.D. founded by an FPU grant awarded by the Spanish Ministry of Universities, supervised by [Juan Lupiáñez](https://www.ugr.es/personal/juan-lupianez-castillo) and [Miguel A. Vadillo](https://mvadillo.com/). My research focuses on the study of the learning conditions of Value-Modulated Attentional Capture (VMAC). Specifically, this thesis aims to test the extent to which we can consider VMAC as an implicit psychological process. 

-   2021--2022 \| **M.S.** *Cogntive and Behavioral Neuroscience*, University of Granada.

-   2016--2020 \| **B.S.** *Psychology*, University of Murcia


## <i class="fa-solid fa-file-lines"></i> Publications

### Journal Artcles
<hr>

Franco-Martínez, A., Rey-Sáez, R., Adrián-Ventura, J., … **Garre-Frutos, F.**, … Vadillo, M. A. (in press). Replicating the unconscious working memory effect: A multisite preregistered study. *Neuroscience of Consciousness*. <https://doi.org/10.1093/nc/niaf046>.

<hr width="10%">

Martínez-López, P., Vázquez-Millán, A., **Garre-Frutos, F.**, & Luque., D. (in press). Assessing the Validity Evidence for Habit Measures Based on Time Pressure. *Behavior Research Methods*. <https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-025-02865-2>.

<hr width="10%">

**Garre-Frutos, F.**, Lupiáñez, J., & Vadillo, M. A. (2025). Value-modulated attentional capture depends on awareness. Psychonomic Bulletin & Review, 32, 3025–3040. [https://doi.org/10.3758/s13423-025-02734-1](https://doi.org/10.3758/s13423-025-02734-1).

<hr width="10%">

González, F., **Garre-Frutos, F.**,  Hinojosa-Aguayo, I., & Hall, G. (2025). Extinction of outcome-specific Pavlovian-to-instrumental transfer (PIT), instrumental outcome devaluation, and reward-related attentional capture are predicted by affect-driven impulsivity. *Learning & Behavior*. [https://doi.org/10.3758/s13420-025-00676-1](https://doi.org/10.3758/s13420-025-00676-1).

<hr width="10%">

**Garre-Frutos, F.**, A. Ariza., & González, F. (2024). The effect of reward and punishment on the extinction of attentional capture elicited by value-related stimuli. *Psychological Research*, *89*, 89. [https://doi.org/10.1007/s00426-025-02115-2](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00426-025-02115-2).

<hr width="10%">

**Garre-Frutos, F.**, Vadillo, M. A., González, F., & Lupiáñez, J. (2024). On the reliability of value-modulated attentional capture: An online replication and multiverse analysis. *Behavior Research Methods, 56*(6), 5986-6003. [https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-023-02329-5](https://doi.org/10.3758/s13428-023-02329-5).

<hr width="10%">

### Submitted papers
<hr>

Franco-Martínez, A., Ferrer-Mendieta, A., Peiro-Lanchares, C., Vadillo, M. A., & **Garre-Frutos, F.** (2025). Choose your own PAS? Quantitative and Qualitative Validity Evidence for the Perceptual Awareness Scale. *PsyArXiv*. [https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/n7zuw_v1](https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/n7zuw_v1). Submitted manuscript.

Theeuwes, J., Manini, G., **Garre-Frutos, F.**, Lupiañez, J., & Van Moorselaar, D. The capture debate revisited: Proactive or reactive suppression—*That is the Question*. Submitted manuscript.


**Garre-Frutos, F.**, Vadillo, M. A. Theeuwes, J., Van Moorselaar, D., & Lupiañez, J.  (2025). The role of selective attention in value-modulated attentional capture. *PsyArXiv*. [https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/kv2rx_v2](https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/kv2rx_v3). Submitted manuscript.

<hr width="10%">

Manini, G.\*, **Garre-Frutos, F.**\*, Botta, F., Martínez-Arévalo, E., & Lupiañez, J. (2025). Facilitation by Irrelevant Distractors Under High Perceptual Load: Suppression or Controlled Attentional Capture? *PsyArXiv*. [https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/xq2h8_v3](https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/xq2h8_v3). Submitted manuscript.

<hr width="10%">

Manini, G., **Garre-Frutos, F.**, Botta, F., Martínez-Arévalo, E., Lupiañez, J. (2024). Distraction vs. Interference: Handling fully irrelevant vs. potentially relevant distractors. *PsyArXiv*. [https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/rkpb8_v1](https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/rkpb8_v1). Submitted manuscript.

<hr width="10%">

**Garre-Frutos, F.**, Lupiañez, J. (2024). Inhibition of Return and Learned Value. *PsyArXiv*. [https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/p4m29](https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/p4m29). Submitted manuscript.

<hr width="10%">

### Working papers
<hr>

**Garre-Frutos, F.**\*, López-Martínez, P\*., Luque, D., Vadillo, M. A., González, F. (2024). Explicit knowledge modulates Pavlovian attentional biases. *In preparation*. 

<hr width="10%">

López-Martínez, P., **Garre-Frutos, F.**, Vadillo, M. A. Luque, D. Lingering habits: Testing the temporal stability of habits. *Data being collected*. 

<hr width="10%">

López-Martínez, P., **Garre-Frutos, F.**, Vadillo, M. A. Luque, D. Reliability and validity of experimental measures of habits. *In preparation*. 

<hr width="10%">

**Garre-Frutos, F.**, Solana, P., López-Martínez, P., Lupiañez, J., González, F., & Vadillo, M. A. Is there value in value-driven distraction? A meta-analysis of studies on individual differences. *In preparation*. 

<hr width="10%">

Rey-Sáez, R., Franco-Martínez, A., Castillejo, A., Vadillo, M. A., & **Garre-Frutos, F.**. A multilevel signal detection approach to unconscious processing research. *In preparation*. 

<hr width="10%">
